messages.content:
Which property is used to change the left margin of an element?
padding-left
margin-left
indent

Which property is used to change the font of an element?
  font-family
  font-style
  font-weight

How do you select an element with id 'demo'?
#demo
demo
. demo
*demo

How do you select elements with class name 'test'?
#test
.test
*test
test

How do you group selectors?
Separate each selector with a plus sign
Separate each selector with a comma
Separate each selector with a space
What is the correct HTML element for inserting a line break?

The correct HTML element for inserting a line break is `
`. This is a self-closing tag and it does not require a closing tag, as it represents a line break or new line within a block of text. Example: ```

First line
Second line

``` This will create two lines of text inside the paragraph, with a line break between them.

What is the correct HTML for adding a background color?
<background> yellow</background>
<body bg="yellow">
<body style="background-color:yellow;">

The correct HTML for adding a background color is: ``` ``` This will set the background color of the entire HTML page to yellow. Alternatively, it's also possible to add a background color to a specific element, using its respective CSS selector: ```

This text has a yellow background.

``` This will set the background color of the `div` element to yellow, including the text inside it.
What is the correct HTML for creating a hyperlink? http://www.w3schools.com/

The correct HTML for creating a hyperlink is: ``` w3schools.com ``` The `a` tag defines a hyperlink, and the `href` attribute specifies the URL of the page the link goes to. The text between the opening and closing `a` tags is the visible link text that the user clicks. Example: ```

Visit w3schools.com for HTML tutorials.

``` This will display a clickable hyperlink with the text "w3schools.com", directing the user to the specified URL.

How can you open a link in a new tab/browser window?
<a href="url" target="_blank">
<a href="url" target="new">
<a href="url" new>

To open a link in a new tab or browser window, you can use the `target` attribute and set its value to `_blank`. Example: ``` Visit W3Schools! ``` This will create a hyperlink to `http://www.w3schools.com/` that will open in a new tab or browser window when clicked. The underscore `_` is used to indicate that the link should open in a new tab or window.

Which of these elements are all <table> elements?
<table> <head> <tfoot>
<thead> <body> <tr>
‹table> <tr> <tt>
<table> <tr> <td>

The correct answer is:

```
<table> <thead> <tfoot>
<table> <tr> <td>
```

The `<table>` element is used to create a table in HTML, and it can contain several other elements to define its structure and content.

`<thead>` and `<tfoot>` elements are used to define the header and footer of a `<table>`, respectively.

`<tr>` is used to define a table row and it works as a container for table cells (`<td>` elements) or table headers (`<th>` elements).

So, only the first and the last options list elements that are all valid `<table>` elements.
